text.title: shadcn/ui: Examples and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Button with cva(), compound Card, dark mode globals.css, and common agent mistakes.

antipattern.description: Agents rewrite `components/ui/button.tsx` to "improve" it — adding features, adjusting styles.
antipattern.reason: ui/ is vendored. Edits are overwritten on the next `shadcn add --overwrite`. Lock the directory in CODEOWNERS and add a CI guard that rejects unallowlisted edits to ui/.

antipattern.description: Agents write hex values into globals.css (`--primary: #2563eb`) instead of HSL triplets.
antipattern.reason: Tailwind's opacity modifier syntax (`bg-primary/90`) requires bare HSL triplets. Hex values break alpha helpers silently — the color renders but transparency does not work.

antipattern.description: Agents add shadcn components to Next.js RSC files without checking for `"use client"`.
antipattern.reason: Most shadcn components use React hooks and require a client boundary. Missing `"use client"` causes a hydration error that reports as an unrelated ReactCurrentDispatcher error.

antipattern.description: Agents use the deprecated `shadcn-ui` package name (`npx shadcn-ui@latest`) instead of `shadcn`.
antipattern.reason: The CLI was renamed to `shadcn`. Training data before the rename uses the old name. Pin the current CLI name in any prompt or scaffold script agents read.
